arguments.task: Open and read the official Higgsfield CLI page at higgsfield.ai/cli. Then navigate only to official Higgsfield first-party pages that document API or CLI authentication, setup, pricing or plans, and any MCP/server documentation. Return the canonical URLs visited and concise extracted evidence for: product description, documented auth methods, whether access is self-serve or gated, API surface type, any rate limit notes, and whether an official MCP exists. Do not rely on search-result snippets. If a page is JS-rendered, wait for content to load; if a page is inaccessible or thin, note that clearly.

raw_result.description: AI image and video generation platform with a CLI and official MCP server for agent-driven creative workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs show browser-based account sign-in for the CLI via `higgsfield auth login`, and the MCP page says no API key is required and users sign in with their Higgsfield account after connecting the MCP server. No first-party page in the gathered evidence explicitly documents API keys, bearer tokens, or HTTP Basic.
raw_result.gating_reason: The official CLI page shows self-serve installation (`npm install -g @higgsfield/cli`), the MCP page provides a public server URL and setup flow, and the pricing page exposes public self-serve plans. Some premium 'Unlimited' and 'Free Generations' benefits are explicitly unavailable via MCP/CLI, but the tools themselves are publicly available without contact-sales or admin approval.

api_surface.breadth: Official first-party evidence supports a CLI and an MCP server for generation workflows, but no standalone REST or GraphQL API documentation was found in the gathered pages; an attempted `/docs` page returned 404.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP page documents an MCP server at https://mcp.higgsfield.ai/mcp with setup for clients including Claude and Cursor.

raw_result.blocker: No documented REST or GraphQL API surface was found; toolkit buildability depends on using the official MCP server or automating the CLI/account sign-in flow rather than a conventional web API.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No numeric API rate limits were found. Pricing evidence indicates credit-based usage, plan-based parallel generation limits, and dynamic speed adjustments during high traffic.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official site prominently documents both a CLI and an MCP integration path. Pricing explicitly states some website-only generation entitlements are not accessible via MCP/CLI, which is an important capability constraint for agents.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field `api_surface.type` is uncertain under the required enum. Evidence clearly supports an official MCP and CLI, but MCP is not one of the allowed API surface enum values, and no first-party REST/GraphQL documentation was found. I mapped this to `None`, but that classification may need human confirmation.

result.description: AI image and video generation platform with a CLI and official MCP server for agent-driven creative workflows.
result.auth_notes: Official docs show browser-based account sign-in for the CLI via `higgsfield auth login`, and the MCP page says no API key is required and users sign in with their Higgsfield account after connecting the MCP server. No first-party page in the gathered evidence explicitly documents API keys, bearer tokens, or HTTP Basic.
result.gating_reason: The official CLI page shows self-serve installation (`npm install -g @higgsfield/cli`), the MCP page provides a public server URL and setup flow, and the pricing page exposes public self-serve plans. Some premium 'Unlimited' and 'Free Generations' benefits are explicitly unavailable via MCP/CLI, but the tools themselves are publicly available without contact-sales or admin approval.
